summary: PII — API rate limiting per tenant (max 1k req/min)
description_md:
As a workspace-admin, I want the PII Auto-Tagging API to enforce rate limits of 1000 requests per minute per tenant, so that no single tenant can overwhelm the compliance vault and degrade service for other tenants.

## Background

High-volume automated clients have been making excessive PII tagging requests, causing increased latency across the workspace. Rate limiting per tenant will ensure fair resource allocation and protect the audit log infrastructure from overload.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Given a tenant has not made any PII tagging requests in the last minute, when they make requests, then the first 1000 requests within that minute window are allowed
2. Given a tenant has made 1000 PII tagging requests within the current minute window, when they make an additional request, then the API returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header
3. Given a tenant exceeded the rate limit, when 60 seconds have passed since their first request in the window, then their request counter resets and they can make new requests
4. Given rate limiting is enabled, when a request is throttled, then the workspace-admin can see the throttled request count in the Connector Health Monitor dashboard

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Implemented using token bucket algorithm with per-tenant tracking. Verified with load tests simulating 5 tenants at 1.2k req/min each. All acceptance criteria passing.
